title: Intense Malaysian Lamb Tagine
description: A pungent and shattering solo-dinner Malaysian dish featuring dried fruit, cooked in a tagine with Mediterranean-friendly flavors
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute
Add the dried apricots and prunes and stir to combine
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the oven
Cook the tagine in a preheated oven at 300 degrees for 2 1/2 hours
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes
Serve the lamb and fruit mixture over couscous or rice
